events.title: 'Haitian solutions for Haitian problems: Every international intervention in Haiti has failed'
events.description: As the Kenyan peace force is set to leave Haiti, Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Rosa Freedman, Professor of Law Conflict and Global Development at the University of Reading. She argues that the crisis in Haiti reflects deep structural failures, not just “gang violence.” She calls the Kenyan mission “a profound failure from the outset” and says “anyone… who understands Haiti knew that this was doomed to failure.” Gangs, she notes, are “not a homogeneous group of criminals” but part of “layers of collusion and corruption.” Ultimately, she urges “Haitian solutions for Haitian problems,” warning that without addressing root causes, including "to remedy the sovereign debt that has left it in this dire financial situation," interventions will keep failing.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Haitian solutions for Haitian problems: Every international intervention in Haiti has failed'

events.title: AI in advertising risks fuelling information crisis, UN warns
events.description: With spending on advertising topping $1 trillion a year worldwide, the United Nations on Wednesday highlighted the untapped power of major brands to shape the future of Artificial Intelligence, warning that a failure to act could deepen a global information integrity crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 AI in advertising risks fuelling information crisis, UN warns

events.title: Global press freedom falls to lowest level in 25 years, RSF warns
events.description: Freedom of the press has fallen to its lowest level in a quarter of a century, NGO Reporters without Borders (RSF) warned Thursday as it released its annual global ranking. The group reported a worldwide decline in media freedom, citing factors ranging from US President Donald Trump’s “systematic” attacks on the press to actions in Saudi Arabia, where a journalist was executed in 2025.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global press freedom falls to lowest level in 25 years, RSF warns

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council
events.description: The recently extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon remains under strain as violence continues, with civilian deaths and injuries reported in Israeli strikes over the weekend. Meanwhile, the Security Council is debating maritime security with the UN chief calling on nations restricting ship movement through the Strait of Hormuz to “let the global economy breathe.” Follow in-depth meeting coverage here. Stay with us for live updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council

events.title: Chokepoints and conflict: How the Hormuz crisis is exposing global shipping vulnerabilities
events.description: The blockading of ships in the Strait of Hormuz as a result of the conflict between the United States and Iran has demonstrated how ships and seafarers have become “leverage in geopolitical disputes,” according to the head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization (IMO).

events.title: Darfur: Two decades on, a new generation of children faces 'horrific violence'
events.description: Twenty years after the conflict in Darfur first sparked global outrage, children in the region are once again trapped in a catastrophic cycle of violence, hunger, and displacement – but this time, the world is failing to take notice.

events.title: Families sue OpenAI over failure to report Canada mass shooter’s behavior on ChatGPT
events.description: New lawsuits allege employees urged company to notify authorities months before deadly Tumbler Ridge attack Families of seven victims of a mass shooting at a secondary school in British Columbia are suing OpenAI and the company’s CEO for negligence after it failed to alert authorities to the shooter’s troubling conversations with ChatGPT. The lawsuits, filed on Wednesday in a federal court in San Francisco, allege that the violent intentions of the shooter, identified as 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar, were well-known to OpenAI. Employees at the company flagged the shooter’s account eight months before the attack and determined that it posed “a credible and specific threat of gun violence against real people”, according to the lawsuit. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Families sue OpenAI over failure to report Canada mass shooter’s behavior on ChatGPT

events.title: ‘Total peace’ or ‘all-out war’? Colombian voters face stark choice as rebel attacks surge
events.description: As the country prepares to elect a new president, a fierce debate is raging on how to end the decades-long armed conflict for good The landmark 2016 peace deal between the Colombian government and the largest insurgent army in Latin America succeeded in some ways: the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc) agreed to lay down their weapons, and the violence that had racked the country was substantially reduced. But the deal alone could not end the decades-long armed conflict for good. Subsequent administrations slow-walked the implementation of the settlement, which was rejected by Farc dissidents and other rebel factions. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Total peace’ or ‘all-out war’? Colombian voters face stark choice as rebel attacks surge

events.title: New IRA bombing fuels fears of global militant network tied to Iran, Hezbollah
events.description: The New IRA reportedly claimed a car bomb attack on a Belfast police station, threatening further strikes amid alleged Iran and Hezbollah connections.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New IRA bombing fuels fears of global militant network tied to Iran, Hezbollah

events.title: Mother says she ‘misses and loves’ daughter after NT girl’s body found – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Warning: This article contains references to Indigenous Australians who have died Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Chalmers understands calls for gas export tax, but says government focused on getting fuel for Australians Chalmers said he understands calls to tax gas exports, but maintained the government was set on securing international supply arrangements during the ongoing fuel crisis. He told ABC News: I understand that there is a constituency in the Australian community to go further … But there are also, as the prime minister said, really good reasons to prioritise these international supply arrangements particularly during this oil shock. All of us have been prioritising getting fuel for Australia and for Australians to keep the economy ticking over and that’s why there are good reasons for the comments that the prime minister made yesterday. We’ve been very upfront with people and said when we think about the intergenerational unfairness in the budget, in our economy, in our society more broadly, a couple of the drivers of that are in housing, are in the tax system. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mother says she ‘misses and loves’ daughter after NT girl’s body found – as it happened

events.title: Russia scales down WW2 victory parade due to Ukrainian threat
events.description: No military hardware will roll through Red Square in Russia's grand World War II victory parade next month -- a precautionary measure given the threat of Ukrainian retaliatory strikes, the Kremlin said Wednesday. The move to a slimmed-down May 9 parade -- typically a bombastic display of Russia's military might -- comes more than four years into Russia's war in Ukraine that has killed hundreds of thousands and drained vast economic resources.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia scales down WW2 victory parade due to Ukrainian threat

events.title: World leaders condemn ‘unacceptable’ violence after armed attack disrupts WH Correspondents’ Dinner
events.description: Global leaders expressed relief that Trump was unharmed after an armed suspect allegedly attacked the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington.

events.title: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
events.description: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle East Three vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry. The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker carrying 18,000 barrels of oil, according to the Maritime Security Centre Indian Ocean (MSCIO), the tracking service of the EU’s naval force. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week

events.title: 2015 nuclear deal ‘no basis’ for any new agreement with Iran

events.title: Anti-gun violence nonprofit founder and ex-MMA fighter convicted in fatal 2024 shooting murder

events.title: ‘Outrageous’: Rebel Wilson denies she was behind Snapchat leak of actor’s nude photo, court hears
events.description: The Pitch Perfect star is being sued for defamation by Charlotte MacInnes, the lead actor of Wilson’s directorial debut, The Deb Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Hollywood star Rebel Wilson has slammed as “outrageous” an accusation she directed a cyber attack on her co-star’s social media account that led to a nude photo leak. The Pitch Perfect star is being sued for defamation by Charlotte MacInnes, the 27-year-old lead actor of the musical comedy The Deb, which is Wilson’s directorial debut. In Australia, support is available at Full Stop Australia (1800 385 578). In the UK, Rape Crisis offers support on 0808 500 2222. In the US, Rainn offers support on 800-656-4673. Other international helplines can be found at ibiblio.org/rcip/internl.html Continue reading...

events.title: Assad regime trials are 'integral for Syria to move on' from civil war, atrocities
events.description: A Syrian court conducted the first hearing Sunday in the trial of ousted ruler Bashar al-Assad and senior figures from his government, one of whom appeared in person. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Nanar Hawach, Senior Analyst for Syria at the International Crisis Group, says that despite most perpetrators being tried in absentia, "transitional justice is integral for Syria to move on past the civil war and past atrocities".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Assad regime trials are 'integral for Syria to move on' from civil war, atrocities

events.description: Spokesperson says just two out of 14 Australians remain in communication, after CCTV footage showed Israeli navy boarding vessels in international waters Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The family of a Newcastle man among at least six Australians onboard boats that were intercepted by Israeli navy personnel in international waters has issued a tearful plea for his safety and lashed out at the Australian government for not intervening. More than 50 boats set sail to Gaza from Italy on Monday as part of the Global Sumud flotilla in the hope of delivering 500 tonnes of aid and volunteers to the Gaza Strip, which remains under naval blockade by Israel. events.title: ‘We’re coming for you’: NT police search for suspect after body of missing five-year-old found
events.description: Mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby, whose body was found after five-day search near Alice Springs, says ‘it’s going to be so hard to live the rest of our lives without you’ • Warning: This article contains references to Indigenous Australians who have died Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Police in the Northern Territory are searching for a man they suspect of murder after finding a body believed to be that of a five-year-old girl who went missing from Alice Springs on Saturday. The NT police commissioner, Martin Dole, told reporters the tragic discovery was made just before noon local time on Thursday, about 5km south of Old Timers town camp, where she was last seen. The girl’s family requested she be referred to as Kumanjayi Little Baby, in line with cultural protocols. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘We’re coming for you’: NT police search for suspect after body of missing five-year-old found

events.title: Student’s alleged jailing in China over Australian pro-democracy protests sparks calls for inquiry
events.description: Human rights commissioner says alleged jailing highlights the ‘growing risks of transnational repression’ in Australia Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s human rights commissioner has said the Chinese student who was allegedly jailed for six years by Chinese authorities for joining protests in Sydney underscores the “very real and growing risks of transnational repression affecting people in Australia – including international students”. Commissioner Lorraine Finlay told Guardian Australia that while she could not comment on the circumstances of individual cases “no one should fear punishment abroad for exercising their lawful rights to free expression and peaceful protest here”. Continue reading...

events.title: Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns
events.description: Kremlin-controlled paramilitaries also alleged it inflicted ‘irreplaceable losses’ on insurgents avoiding civilian casualties Russia’s defence ministry has claimed its Africa Corps – the successor to the former Wagner mercenary group – prevented a coup in Mali over the weekend, avoiding mass civilian casualties and inflicting “irreplaceable losses” on rebel insurgents. It said in a statement that its troops in the desert town of Kidal near the Algerian border had fought for more than 24 hours while completely surrounded and vastly outnumbered. It also alleged, without providing evidence, that the militants had been trained by European mercenary instructors, including Ukrainians. The casualty toll was not specified. Continue reading...

But as his term draws to a close – with the first round of a presidential election set for May 31 – Petro’s ambitious energy transition plan remains unfulfilled, with the country still reliant on fossil-fuel production.

events.title: Trump administration urges countries to help citizens stuck in Syria as Australia maintains hardline stance

events.title: ‘It will never cover what’s authentic’: African music industry weighs up AI risks and rewards
events.description: Delegates at event in Cape Verde highlight opportunities from tech while stressing AI is no replacement for talent Last July, the Nigerian singer-songwriter Fave found herself caught up in a viral moment: an unauthorised version of a track by her featuring an AI choir had been released, quickly becoming an internet sensation. To get ahead of the situation, she recorded her own remix that integrated the AI-assisted song and added it to her discography. “In my view, [that] was smart and very business aware,” Oyinkansola Fawehinmi, a Lagos-based entertainment lawyer, observed a few months later . “She essentially reclaimed the ‘AI version’ and released it as her own official expression.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘It will never cover what’s authentic’: African music industry weighs up AI risks and rewards

events.title: Christchurch gunman fails in bid to appeal against guilty pleas in New Zealand court
events.description: Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslims said poor mental health made him admit to crimes The Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslim worshippers at two mosques in Christchurch in 2019 has been prevented from appealing against his guilty pleas, after one of New Zealand’s highest courts said his bid was “utterly devoid of merit”. Brenton Tarrant, who is responsible for the worst mass shooting in New Zealand’s history, asked the court of appeal in February to allow him to appeal against his guilty pleas, claiming harsh prison conditions had affected his mental health and compelled him to admit to the crimes. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Christchurch gunman fails in bid to appeal against guilty pleas in New Zealand court

Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, 28, and his cousin Tobias Mugabe Matonhodze, 33, were initially both charged with attempted murder after the incident on 19 February. Continue reading...

events.title: More private health records of UK Biobank volunteers appear on Chinese website

Addressing a House of Lords debate on the attempted sale of data belonging to 500,000 UK Biobank volunteers, Patrick Vallance said the government had worked with Chinese officials to remove additional postings on the online marketplace. events.title: US charges Sinaloa governor and other Mexican officials with drug trafficking offences
events.description: Indictment accuses high-level officials in Sinaloa of offences such as drug trafficking, weapons offences and kidnapping The US justice department has charged the governor of Sinaloa and nine other current and former officials for alleged ties to the Sinaloa cartel, accusing them of aiding in the massive importation of illicit narcotics into the United States . Some officials were members of Mexico’s progressive ruling party, Morena, posing a political conundrum for Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum as she seeks to offset mounting pressures from the Trump administration. events.title: US supreme court conservatives seem to favor ending TPS for Haitians and Syrians
events.description: Nine justices were hearing Trump administration that it has authority to strip immigrants’ temporary protected status The US supreme court heard oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of immigrant Haitians and Syrians, under a program that has shielded them from deportation owing to safety concerns in their countries of origin. During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration’s attempts to strip humanitarian protections for the Syrians and Haitians in this case. Continue reading...

events.title: Kim praises North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up to evade Ukraine capture

Mounting evidence, including from intelligence reports and testimonies of defectors, has indicated North Korean soldiers are explicitly told to resort to self-detonation or other forms of suicide to avoid falling into enemy hands. events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
events.description: No description available.

events.title: M 4.0 - 1 km WNW of Cooter, Missouri
events.description: PAGER - GREEN ShakeMap - IV DYFI? - IV Time 2026-04-23 17:59:04 UTC 2026-04-23 17:59:04 UTC at epicenter Location 36.055°N 89.826°W Depth 11.78 km (7.32 mi)

events.title: Activists say Israel has intercepted their Gaza aid flotilla near Crete
events.description: Activists sailing on dozens of boats attempting to break Israel's blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver aid say Israeli forces intercepted them, detaining the crews while the flotilla was sailing near the Greek island of Crete.

events.title: The Austrian nuns who fled their care home are now in Rome and visited the Vatican
events.description: The three octogenarian nuns, who made headlines last year after they broke back into their convent, joined others at St. Peter's Square for a general audience with Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday morning.

events.title: South Korean court sentences ex-President Yoon to 7 years in prison
events.description: An appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024.

events.title: EU says Meta is failing to keep underage users off Facebook and Instagram
events.description: The European Union accused Meta on Wednesday of failing to stop underage users from accessing Facebook and Instagram, in violation of the bloc's digital rules that require sites to protect minors.

events.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
events.description: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

events.title: Women journalists in Ghana encounter – and overcome – gender barriers
events.description: In Ghana, DW Akademie works with women journalists as they try to advance in their careers despite gender barriers.

events.title: Ecuador starts fracking in Amazon rainforest
events.description: Ecuador is pushing to increase its production of crude oil but faces a backlash from environmentalists and Indigenous groups.

events.title: King Charles commemorates 9/11 victims in New York visit
events.description: The royal couple laid a bouquet at the September 11 Memorial and met with victim's families.

events.title: US top court mulls scrapping Syrians', Haitians' protection
events.description: The Trump administration is appealing lower court orders that blocked it from immediately ending temporary protected status for people from Haiti and Syria.

events.title: Germany: Merz hails 'historic' health care reform
events.description: The Cabinet has greenlit a health care draft law designed to lower insurance rates. The bill heads to parliament next, but doctors' associations and patient protection advocates say it amounts to austerity measures.

events.title: Stalemate in Madrid as resilient Arsenal blunt Atletico Madrid in semi-final clash
events.description: After a tight match, the Gunners and the Colchoneros parted on good terms with the score at 1–1, and will battle it out for a place in the final in London next Tuesday. Both goals in the match were scored from the penalty spot.

events.title: New York's Mamdani calls on King Charles to 'return' Koh-i-Noor diamond taken from India
events.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani on Wednesday called on King Charles III to "return" the Koh-i-Noor diamond, a 105.6 carat gem that was mined in India and is now the star of Britain's crown jewels. The diamond has been in British hands since 1849 but its ownership is contested with several countries laying claim.

events.title: Trump says NASA has ‘good shot’ at returning astronauts to Moon before 2029
events.description: US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday he believes NASA has a “good shot” at returning astronauts to the Moon before 2029, citing progress on the Artemis II programme, though he cautioned against guaranteeing success during his second term in office.

events.title: Arsenal, Atletico Madrid share draw after penalty drama in Champions League semi-final
events.description: Julian Alvarez scored a penalty as Atletico Madrid held Arsenal to a 1-1 draw in their UEFA Champions League semi-final first leg on Wednesday. Viktor Gyokeres had put Arsenal ahead from the spot before Alvarez equalised, while a late penalty call for Arsenal was overturned after a VAR review.

events.title: Saudi Arabia to withdraw LIV Golf funding
events.description: LIV Golf players and staff have been told that Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund is withdrawing investment from its costly breakaway series after the 2026 season, Sky News understands.

events.title: Cartel leader tipped to succeed 'El Mencho' captured
events.description: Mexico's army has captured a cartel leader tipped to succeed notorious drug lord "El Mencho".

events.title: Myanmar reduces ousted leader Suu Kyi's prison term in new amnesty
events.description: Myanmar’s military-backed government shortened the prison sentence of ousted leader Aung San Suu Kyi

events.title: Activists say Israel has intercepted their Gaza aid flotilla near Crete, detaining crews
events.description: Activists attempting to break Israel’s maritime blockade of Gaza say Israeli forces have intercepted their flotilla near the southern Greek island of Crete

events.title: French teen charged in Singapore over a vending machine straw-licking video
events.description: A French teen faces charges in Singapore after posting a video of himself licking a straw from an orange juice vending machine and putting it back

events.title: Brazil's Senate blocks Lula's Supreme Court nominee, first rejection in 132 years
events.description: Brazil’s Senate dealt a political blow to President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Wednesday by rejecting his appointee to the Supreme Court, the first in more than 130 years

events.title: Canada will be the headquarters for a future NATO-linked financial institution, official says
events.description: A senior government official says that Canada has been selected as the headquarters for a new, financial institution led by NATO and designed to reduce borrowing costs for members of the alliance

events.title: Brazil's Senate rejects president's Supreme Court nominee for first time in more than 100 years
events.description: Brazil's Senate rejects president's Supreme Court nominee for first time in more than 100 years

events.title: What to know about the pro-Russian activist arrested in South Africa and wanted in Benin
events.description: Pro-Russian activist Kémi Séba, who is accused of backing a failed coup in Benin, is due in court in South Africa on Wednesday after being arrested alongside his son

events.title: Thailand ex-PM Thaksin is set for prison release next month after getting parole
events.description: Former Thai Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra is set to be released from a Bangkok prison next month after a Justice Ministry panel decision to grant him parole

events.title: He once struck a peace deal with Israel and says Lebanon's leaders should try again now
events.description: Former Lebanese President Amin Gemayel believes the time might be right to pursue peace talks with Israel

events.title: Georgia man says he suffered severe medical neglect leading to amputations at scrutinized Atlanta jail
events.description: A Georgia man said he lost his fingers and lower legs to amputation after allegedly being denied critical medical care at the Fulton County Jail.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Georgia man says he suffered severe medical neglect leading to amputations at scrutinized Atlanta jail

events.title: Illegal immigrant admitted posing as Border Patrol agent in calculated scheme to disrupt deportations: DOJ
events.description: A man accused of impersonating a Border Patrol agent to interfere with ICE operations pleaded guilty and faces up to 15 years on gun charges.

events.title: Suspect arrested after mom, pregnant teen, 12-year-old found bound and murdered
events.description: Authorities arrested William Graham Oliver in the murders of three family members found bound in an Alabama home, including two children.

events.title: Louisiana sheriff charged after toilet jailbreak let 10 inmates escape in lockup fiasco
events.description: Louisiana Sheriff Susan Hutson was indicted on 30 counts, including obstruction of justice and malfeasance, stemming from a major New Orleans jailbreak.

events.title: Kohberger defense team says it is 'appalled' by former expert's public comments about Idaho murders case
events.description: Bryan Kohberger's defense team says former expert Brent Turvey broke his confidentiality agreement by speaking publicly about the Idaho murders case. He denies wrongdoing.

events.title: Mississippi man charged with killing mother, allegedly flushing her flesh down toilet after dismemberment
events.description: Zachary Jackson Jr. faces murder charges after allegedly killing his mother and flushing her remains in a Natchez, Mississippi home, authorities say.

events.title: Sexual predator ‘Cookie Monster’ caught in terrified child’s bed during midnight break-in, police say
events.description: A 65-year-old repeat sex offender allegedly entered a child's bedroom naked. He is now held on $1.25 million bond in Douglas County Corrections.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Sexual predator ‘Cookie Monster’ caught in terrified child’s bed during midnight break-in, police say

events.title: Iran’s $800M oil smuggling scheme uses tankers posing as Iraqi ships to dodge blockade
events.description: Sanctioned tankers are allegedly disguising Iranian oil shipments as Iraqi cargo while Trump doubles down on the naval blockade to squeeze Tehran.

events.title: Record antisemitic incidents in Canada fuel criticism of Carney government response
events.description: Canada faces record antisemitic incidents in 2025 as critics say Prime Minister Mark Carney's government is failing to adequately address anti-Jewish hate.

events.title: Could Narges Mohammadi unite Iran’s opposition? Husband says imprisoned Nobel laureate still fighting
events.description: Narges Mohammadi's husband says the jailed Nobel Peace Prize laureate was severely beaten during arrest but remains politically unbroken in prison.
